pi_items.source: task.json description states 'Create a new standard collection named **Theoretical Foundations**'

Code analysis of seed_library.py (CLASSIC_PAPERS) reveals exactly 3 papers with 'Theory' in title: (1) 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Shannon, 1948), (2) 'An Unsolvable Problem of Elementary Number Theory' (Church, 1936), (3) 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Shannon & Weaver, 1949). Field ID 1 search in setup_task.sh confirms title field search for 'Theory' keyword

seed_library.py line 10 documents field mapping: 'Field IDs: title=1'. Code at line 770 shows field_map = {1: title, ...}, confirming fieldID 1 is the title field. Both setup_task.sh and export_result.sh use 'WHERE d.fieldID = 1' to query titles

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data from seed_library.py in 'all' mode, which seeds 10 classic papers and 8 ML papers. Exactly 3 papers contain 'Theory' in the Title field (fieldID=1): Shannon's 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' (1948), Shannon & Weaver's 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' (1949), and Church's 'An Unsolvable Problem of Elementary Number Theory' (1936). The target collection 'Theoretical Foundations' must be a standard Collection (not a Saved Search) containing these 3 items for full task completion.
